Several top executives at Phreesia have recently sold significant amounts of company stock. GC & Secretary Allison Hoffman sold 6,585 shares for $111,945, while CEO Chaim Indig sold 8,440 shares for $143,480. President, Provider Solutions Evan Roberts sold 6,317 shares for $107,389, and President, Network Solutions David Linetsky sold 6,106 shares for $103,802. SVP HR Amy Beth VanDuyn sold 2,602 shares for $44,234. CFO Balaji Gandhi completed one of the larger transactions, selling 8,009 shares for $136,153. In the last 24 hours, Phreesia has seen a cluster of analyst actions driven more by evolving views of its growth and profitability profile than by any single new data point. RBC Capital resumed coverage, citing the company’s pivot toward profitability and the potential to accelerate growth and expand margins by more fully monetizing its patient-intake network across thousands of physicians and broader healthcare utilization. Wells Fargo initiated coverage emphasizing Phreesia’s ownership of key workflows (intake, scheduling, payments) for a sizable share of U.S. patient visits and arguing that its revenue growth since IPO is not reflected in the current valuation, which they no longer see as a major overhang. Truist also reduced its target, noting that the stock underperformed in 2025 despite management’s deliberate shift from pure growth to earnings strength and highlighting persistent investor skepticism about the durability of revenue growth; it now views Phreesia as needing multiple quarters of revenue and EBITDA outperformance to restore confidence. Overall, the news flow reflects a tension between improving profitability and platform strength on one hand and macro health-tech weakness and growth skepticism on the other, which has contributed to recent volatility in Phreesia’s share price.

data.title: Wave of Insider Moves as PTC Therapeutics Leaders Cash In on Company Stock
data.description:
New insider activity at PTC Therapeutics ( (PTCT) ) has taken place on January 12, 2026.

Claim 70% Off TipRanks Premium

  • Unlock hedge fund-level data and powerful investing tools for smarter, sharper decisions
  • Stay ahead of the market with the latest news and analysis and maximize your portfolio's potential

Several top executives at PTC Therapeutics have recently reduced their holdings in the company through notable stock sales. Chief Business Officer Eric Pauwels sold 1,454 shares for a total of $111,158, while Executive Vice President and Chief Legal Officer Mark Elliott Boulding sold 1,286 shares valued at $98,314. Chief Technical Operations Officer Neil Gregory Almstead disposed of 940 shares for $71,863, and EVP & Chief Medical Officer Lee Scott Golden sold 866 shares for $66,205. Senior Vice President & Chief Accounting Officer Christine Marie Utter sold 1,034 shares, generating $79,049. The largest disclosed transaction came from CEO Matthew B. Klein, who sold 2,514 shares for proceeds of $192,195, underscoring a broad wave of insider selling across the company’s leadership team.

Recent Updates on PTCT stock

Over the last 24 hours, PTC Therapeutics’ stock action has been driven mainly by stronger-than-expected preliminary 2025 results and a supportive shift in analyst sentiment. The company pre-announced about $823.4 million in 2025 product and royalty revenue, coming in above prior guidance, with particularly strong momentum from the newly launched Sephience, which delivered robust fourth-quarter sales and rapid patient uptake. In addition, PTC reported ending 2025 with roughly $1.94 billion in cash and securities, helped by fully monetizing its Evrysdi royalty interest via a sale to Royalty Pharma, which boosts near-term liquidity but removes future royalty income. The firm also highlighted regulatory and pipeline progress, including FDA alignment on a Phase 3 Huntington’s trial for votoplam and continued engagement on vatiquinone and Translarna, while 2026 guidance signaled ongoing heavy R&D and SG&A investment as it works toward cash-flow breakeven. Against this backdrop, Morgan Stanley raised its price target on PTCT, citing a favorable outlook for small- and mid-cap commercial biotechs expected to transition from being capital consumers to capital producers, and viewing PTC’s commercial trajectory and cash position as well-positioned relative to large biopharma peers facing patent cliffs, even as balance-sheet leverage and technical factors remain watch points. Recent AI-driven assessments also emphasize strong operational momentum and an undervalued earnings profile as positives, tempered by leverage and free-cash-flow risks.

Spark’s Take on PTCT Stock

According to Spark, TipRanks’ AI Analyst, PTCT is a Neutral.

The score is driven by strong operational momentum (high TTM revenue growth, strong margins, and positive earnings-call guidance around Sephience) and an inexpensive P/E. Offsetting factors are balance-sheet risk from negative equity/high leverage and only neutral technical momentum.

data.description:
New insider activity at Clene ( (CLNN) ) has taken place on January 12, 2026.

Claim 70% Off TipRanks Premium

  • Unlock hedge fund-level data and powerful investing tools for smarter, sharper decisions
  • Stay ahead of the market with the latest news and analysis and maximize your portfolio's potential

Significant Clene shareholder Chidozie Ugwumba has trimmed his position in the company, selling 44,975 shares of Clene stock in a transaction valued at $256,235. This move represents a notable cash realization by the investor and adds fresh data for market watchers tracking insider and major shareholder activity around Clene.

Recent Updates on CLNN stock

In the last 24 hours, Clene’s stock action has been driven mainly by regulatory and financing developments around its ALS candidate CNM‑Au8 rather than by new earnings data. The company announced that the FDA has granted it an in‑person Type C meeting in Q1 2026 to review biomarker data underpinning a potential NDA for CNM‑Au8 in ALS, including statistically significant reductions in NfL and GFAP and new large‑cohort analyses linking modest NfL declines with meaningfully lower mortality risk. Exploratory HEALEY ALS Platform Trial findings also suggest improved survival and a sharply reduced mortality risk in patients showing declines in the IGFBP7 biomarker, aligning with genetic evidence that lower IGFBP7 may protect against ALS progression; these data are central to analysts’ reassessments because they strengthen the case for using NfL and related markers as surrogate endpoints that could support accelerated approval. At the same time, a newly announced $28M+ registered direct offering, with complex warrant structures tied to future FDA milestones and stock price levels, highlights both Clene’s ongoing financial strain and its need to fund operations and potential commercialization, which weighs on sentiment despite the clinical momentum. Overall, analyst and AI-based views cited in the articles reflect a tension between the company’s weak financial profile and bearish technicals versus the potentially transformative impact of favorable FDA engagement and biomarker‑driven ALS data on CNM‑Au8’s regulatory and commercial outlook, which is the key rationale behind recent price target and rating adjustments rather than any short‑term trading move.

Spark’s Take on CLNN Stock

According to Spark, TipRanks’ AI Analyst, CLNN is a Neutral.

Clene’s overall stock score is primarily impacted by its poor financial performance and valuation, reflecting significant financial instability and ongoing losses. The technical analysis indicates bearish momentum, further weighing down the score.

Barclays trimmed its target as it reset software valuations for 2026, noting that while macro and IT spending appear stable and software valuations are relatively low, the sector remains out of favor, prompting a more tempered stance despite expecting a favorable setup ahead. Jefferies lowered its target while emphasizing that 2026 is likely to be another year of only gradual AI monetization; the firm highlighted investor worries about AI potentially disintermediating traditional software and argued that Elastic and peers will need clearer, faster AI-driven growth contributions to dispel those concerns. RBC Capital also cut its target, explaining that 2026 should be when AI tailwinds become more visible for well-positioned enterprise AI players like Elastic, but that overall investor caution persists due to the “AI is the death of software” narrative; the bank cited stabilizing enterprise IT budgets and selective improvement, especially around GenAI use cases, even as management teams remain conservative in their early 2026 guidance. Together, these views frame Elastic’s near-term trading as driven less by company-specific news and more by evolving expectations around AI monetization, sector valuations, and the timing of an eventual improvement in software sentiment.

Spark’s Take on ESTC Stock

According to Spark, TipRanks’ AI Analyst, ESTC is a Neutral.

Elastic’s overall stock score is driven by strong earnings call performance and positive corporate events, indicating strategic growth and shareholder value initiatives. However, the stock faces challenges in technical analysis and valuation due to bearish trends and negative profitability metrics. Addressing these areas could enhance the stock’s attractiveness.

Sky Quarry, Inc. ( (SKYQ) ) has issued an announcement.

On January 12, 2026, Sky Quarry Inc. entered into a Controlled Equity Offering Sales Agreement with Cantor Fitzgerald & Co. that allows the company, at its discretion, to issue and sell up to $4.7 million of its common stock through at-the-market or other permitted transactions, with Cantor acting as principal and/or sales agent and earning a commission of up to 3% on gross proceeds. The shares, which may be sold from time to time under an effective shelf registration statement and related prospectus supplement filed with the SEC, give Sky Quarry additional flexibility to raise equity capital, although the company is under no obligation to sell any shares and retains the right to suspend or terminate the offering, potentially affecting future dilution and funding options for existing shareholders and other stakeholders.

Gyrodyne Company Of America ( (GYRO) ) has shared an update.

GSD Flowerfield LLC, a wholly owned subsidiary of Gyrodyne, LLC, and B2K Smithtown LLC have entered into a Second Amendment dated January 6, 2026, to their July 30, 2025 Purchase and Sale Agreement for Lots 1 and 3 of the Flowerfield property, under which Gyrodyne will credit B2K $1.52 million at closing for specified on-site improvements while B2K assumes responsibility for all common facilities and offsite work. The amendment confirms that B2K’s investigation period and related termination right have expired, clarifies the handling of title objections raised in December 2025, and sets a February 5–6, 2026 window to resolve third-party land use covenants, after which B2K must either terminate and reclaim its deposit or be deemed to accept the covenants, leaving most contract terms unchanged and prompting Gyrodyne to note that any impact on the Flowerfield property’s value and liquidation net assets is not yet determinable.

More about Gyrodyne Company Of America

Gyrodyne, LLC, through its wholly owned subsidiary GSD Flowerfield LLC, is a New York-based real estate owner and developer focused on the Flowerfield property, where it sells and entitles subdivided lots for development, including Lots 1 and 3 that are subject to a sale agreement with B2K Smithtown LLC.
